name: Lionel Hampton
abstract: Lionel Leo Hampton (April 20, 1908 – August 31, 2002) was an American jazz vibraphonist, percussionist, and bandleader. He worked with jazz musicians from Teddy Wilson, Benny Goodman, and Buddy Rich to Charlie Parker, Charles Mingus, and Quincy Jones.

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: So he used to play the Mozart concerto for flute on saxophone and the Webern concerto for clarinet. But then he played the Benny Goodman version of the Mozart concerto for clarinet, back to back with Benny Goodman at Carnegie Hall with Ziggy Eman, Gene Krupa and Teddy Wilson and Lionel Hampton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: So I stayed with the band for a whole year... Hamp would come and start playing two fingers at the top... Lionel Hampton says, 'Gates, I’m going to take you to court, I’m going to sue you, I told you to stay with me'
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: We were one of the first electrified bands there was, was Lionel Hampton's Orchestra... My brother Ernie and Jack McVea and I, we left the band and went into Lionel's band... I had his band all set out for him

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: Hampton and I did a television show together. I had a television series I was the host of, and he came as a guest. And he played first of all, then we got together and we did “Seven Come Eleven” together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Vince DiMartino
about_quotes.evidence: when I played with Clark — with Lionel Hampton when I was, you know like 19... Jerome Richardson came one time to be in Lionel Hampton’s big band... And I was playing with Lionel Hampton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince DiMartino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 8, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: I joined Lionel Hampton’s band from Les Hite’s band. And I was in Lionel Hampton’s band in 1943... when I came out I went back with Lionel Hampton
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
